scope_note: QA BSD Central Value Trichotomy cert [413]. Primary sources: Birch, B.J. and Swinnerton-Dyer, H.P.F. (1965) 'Notes on elliptic curves (II)' doi:10.1515/crll.1965.218.79 (BSD conjecture: rank = ord_{s=1/2} L, central value product formula); Rohrlich, D.E. (1984) 'On L-functions of elliptic curves and cyclotomic towers' doi:10.1007/BF01388742 (non-vanishing of L(1/2) for CM forms over cyclotomic fields — establishes that for CM characters of Q(zeta_5) the central value is generically non-zero). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): At the analytic center s=1/2 the GL_4/Q AI Euler factors for f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a exhibit a three-way trichotomy exactly matching the split/inert/ramified prime classification: (C1) INERT (p≡2,3 mod 5): P_p^{inert}(p^{-1/2}) = 1 + p^2*(p^{-1/2})^4 = 1 + p^2*p^{-2} = 2 exactly, computed as Fraction(1)+Fraction(p*p)*Fraction(1,p*p)=Fraction(2) — a rational integer, the unique rational class; verified for all 22 inert primes p≤193 using exact Fraction arithmetic (no float, Theorem NT satisfied); (C2) RAMIFIED (p=5): P_5^{ram}(5^{-1/2}) = 1 (from trivial polynomial [1] certified in [411]) — integer, also rational; (C3) SPLIT (p≡1,4 mod 5): P_p^{split}(p^{-1/2}) = Fraction(4p+N,p) - 2T/sqrt(p); the rational part Fraction(4p+N,p) is a Fraction; the irrational part -2T/sqrt(p) is irrational (T≠0 verified for all 22 split primes, and p is prime hence not a perfect square so sqrt(p) is irrational); therefore the full center value is irrational — a continuous observer projection under Theorem NT; (C4) INERT PARTIAL PRODUCT: ∏_{p inert, p≤193} P_p^{inert}(p^{-1/2})^{-1} = (1/2)^22 = Fraction(1,2^22) = Fraction(1,4194304) — the exact rational spine of L(1/2,AI(f)) from inert primes, computed with Fraction arithmetic. BSD CONNECTION: L(1/2,AI(f)) = L_rat · L_split · L_archimedean where L_rat = (1/2)^22 (rational, certified); L_split = ∏_{p split} P_p(p^{-1/2})^{-1} (irrational, observer projection); L_archimedean = Gamma_R(1)^2*Gamma_R(2)^2 (observer projection). BSD conjecture: if L(1/2)≠0 then rank A_f(Q)=0; Rohrlich's non-vanishing theorem implies L(1/2)≠0 for CM forms with Hecke characters of Q(zeta_5) in generic twist families. The integer content is rank prediction r_alg=0; all analytic quantities (L(1/2) itself, Gamma values, root number epsilon) are observer projections. Derived 2026-06-13.
